steps.title: Find the radius
steps.content:
Point $A$ is on the circle, so the distance from $C(h,k)$ to $A(h+2,k+\sqrt{113})$ is a radius.
$$r=\sqrt{(h+2-h)^2+(k+\sqrt{113}-k)^2}$$
$$r=\sqrt{2^2+(\sqrt{113})^2}=\sqrt{117}$$

steps.title: Use the right angle
steps.content:
Point $B$ is also on the circle, so $CB$ has the same length as $CA$. Since angle $ACB$ is a right angle, $AB$ is the hypotenuse.
$$AB^2=(\sqrt{117})^2+(\sqrt{117})^2=234$$
$$AB=\sqrt{234}$$
The answer is A.
